human_verification.notes: Validated child-care-specific attendance and billing boundaries, Head Start and CCDF source grounding, denial and mismatch handling, unsupported override refusal, and deployment and commercialization completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to child-care attendance, billing-readiness, posting support, reconciliation, and financial exception handling rather than generic accounting or claims work.

Authority sources anchor the pack in Head Start attendance and fee rules, CCDF payment and co-payment guidance, Office of Child Care improper-payment controls, and jurisdiction-specific licensing retrieval.
Scenarios validate unsupported billing days, missing posting documentation, remittance mismatches, denial handling, mixed-funding boundaries, and unsupported override requests.
The package requires named child-care records such as sign-in/out logs, voucher spans, family fee ledgers, funded-enrollment context, and remittance files before the lane can act.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the package artifacts.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.

notes: The specialist is validated as a spec pack and does not claim retained-implementation runtime behavior.

Promotion to a retained implementation should happen only after repeated production-like usage confirms stable retrieval of attendance, subsidy, family fee, and remittance records with low false-confidence escalation rates.
